title: 1981-04-16_Pat_157.txt
key_claims: Dick shifts his focus to the "inner-outer" feedback loop rather than "present-future." He realizes his experience was the assimilation of world into self, expanding his identity to cosmic proportions by realizing the outer world was just his own "prior thought-formations."

This leads to a profound Neo-Platonist revelation: anamnesis is the immortal soul remembering its own eternal, unfallen reality among the Forms. It is not just remembering past lives, but remembering what one *is*.

Because all concentric realms of emanation exist simultaneously *here* and *now* (as Plotinus taught), remembering oneself automatically transports one from the spatial/temporal realm to the eternal realm.
Valis is ultimately identified as Plotinus' "One," the transcendent unity above and behind the multiplicity of the Forms.
recurring_concepts: **Firebright**: Re-introduced here as the immortal soul, ascending and descending through the realms, who finally wakes up to her true vastness and eternal nature.
reading_excerpt: Dick shifts his focus to the "inner-outer" feedback loop rather than "present-future." He realizes his experience was the assimilation of world into self, expanding his identity to cosmic proportions by realizing the outer world was just his own "prior thought-formations."
